qas.q: DODm 4500.36 management and use of dod non-tactical vehicles

qas.a: inform workers on how to reduce the risk of exposure to chemicals in the work place.
use your legs not your back consider using someone to help you

You should stay at least two seconds
behind any vehicle that is directly in front of your
vehicle, then add more time if driving conditions
become worse, such as night driving or wet/icy road

This program
is governed by OSHA standards and AFI 91-203,
Air Force Occupational Safety, Fire, and Health Standards

The AF 1118,
Notice of Hazard , in the context of the LOTO program, is a danger sign used to permanently identify the existence of a hazardous condition that could result in death, disability, or a mishap.

The AF 979, Danger Tag , is a temporary means of identifying the same conditions and can be issued
by the supervisor as well as a qualified ground safety,
fire prevention, or health officials. The danger
tag provides a means for workplace supervision to immediately alert employees to a real or perceived
danger.
